# stripe_terminal_reader (Resource)

A Reader represents a physical device for accepting payment details.

Related guide: [Connecting to a reader](https://docs.stripe.com/terminal/payments/connect-reader)



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `registration_code` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) A code generated by the reader used for registering to an account.

### Optional

- `label` (String) Custom label given to the reader for easier identification.
- `location` (String) The location identifier of the reader.
- `metadata` (Map of String) Set of [key-value pairs](https://docs.stripe.com/api/metadata) that you can attach to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format.

### Read-Only

- `action` (Attributes) The most recent action performed by the reader. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action))
- `device_sw_version` (String) The current software version of the reader.
- `device_type` (String) Device type of the reader.
- `id` (String) Unique identifier for the object.
- `ip_address` (String) The local IP address of the reader.
- `livemode` (Boolean) If the object exists in live mode, the value is `true`. If the object exists in test mode, the value is `false`.
- `object` (String) String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same value.
- `serial_number` (String) Serial number of the reader.
- `status` (String) The networking status of the reader. We do not recommend using this field in flows that may block taking payments.

<a id="nestedatt--action"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action`

Read-Only:

- `api_error` (Attributes) The reader action failed due to an [API error](https://docs.stripe.com/api/errors). Only present when `status` is `failed` and the underlying failure was an API error. Avoid parsing the `message` field for programmatic logic; use `type` or `code` instead. The `message` field is for display to humans only and may be updated at anytime. Requires [reader version](https://docs.stripe.com/terminal/readers/stripe-reader-s700-s710#reader-software-version) 2.42 or later. Readers on older versions always return null. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--api_error))
- `collect_inputs` (Attributes) Represents a reader action to collect customer inputs (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--collect_inputs))
- `collect_payment_method` (Attributes) Represents a reader action to collect a payment method (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--collect_payment_method))
- `confirm_payment_intent` (Attributes) Represents a reader action to confirm a payment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--confirm_payment_intent))
- `failure_code` (String) Failure code, only set if status is `failed`.
- `failure_message` (String) Detailed failure message, only set if status is `failed`.
- `print_content` (Attributes) Represents a reader action to print content (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--print_content))
- `process_payment_intent` (Attributes) Represents a reader action to process a payment intent (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--process_payment_intent))
- `process_setup_intent` (Attributes) Represents a reader action to process a setup intent (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--process_setup_intent))
- `refund_payment` (Attributes) Represents a reader action to refund a payment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--refund_payment))
- `set_reader_display` (Attributes) Represents a reader action to set the reader display (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--set_reader_display))
- `status` (String) Status of the action performed by the reader.
- `type` (String) Type of action performed by the reader.

<a id="nestedatt--action--api_error"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.api_error`

Read-Only:

- `advice_code` (String) For card errors resulting from a card issuer decline, a short string indicating [how to proceed with an error](https://docs.stripe.com/declines#retrying-issuer-declines) if they provide one.
- `charge` (String) For card errors, the ID of the failed charge.
- `code` (String) For some errors that could be handled programmatically, a short string indicating the [error code](https://docs.stripe.com/error-codes) reported.
- `decline_code` (String) For card errors resulting from a card issuer decline, a short string indicating the [card issuer's reason for the decline](https://docs.stripe.com/declines#issuer-declines) if they provide one.
- `doc_url` (String) A URL to more information about the [error code](https://docs.stripe.com/error-codes) reported.
- `message` (String) A human-readable message providing more details about the error. For card errors, these messages can be shown to your users.
- `network_advice_code` (String) For card errors resulting from a card issuer decline, a 2 digit code which indicates the advice given to merchant by the card network on how to proceed with an error.
- `network_decline_code` (String) For payments declined by the network, an alphanumeric code which indicates the reason the payment failed.
- `param` (String) If the error is parameter-specific, the parameter related to the error. For example, you can use this to display a message near the correct form field.
- `payment_intent` (String) A PaymentIntent guides you through the process of collecting a payment from your customer.
We recommend that you create exactly one PaymentIntent for each order or
customer session in your system. You can reference the PaymentIntent later to
see the history of payment attempts for a particular session.

A PaymentIntent transitions through
[multiple statuses](/payments/paymentintents/lifecycle)
throughout its lifetime as it interfaces with Stripe.js to perform
authentication flows and ultimately creates at most one successful charge.

Related guide: [Payment Intents API](https://docs.stripe.com/payments/payment-intents)
- `payment_method` (String) PaymentMethod objects represent your customer's payment instruments.
You can use them with [PaymentIntents](https://docs.stripe.com/payments/payment-intents) to collect payments or save them to
Customer objects to store instrument details for future payments.

Related guides: [Payment Methods](https://docs.stripe.com/payments/payment-methods) and [More Payment Scenarios](https://docs.stripe.com/payments/more-payment-scenarios).
- `payment_method_type` (String) If the error is specific to the type of payment method, the payment method type that had a problem. This field is only populated for invoice-related errors.
- `request_log_url` (String) A URL to the request log entry in your dashboard.
- `setup_intent` (String) A SetupIntent guides you through the process of setting up and saving a customer's payment credentials for future payments.
For example, you can use a SetupIntent to set up and save your customer's card without immediately collecting a payment.
Later, you can use [PaymentIntents](https://api.stripe.com#payment_intents) to drive the payment flow.

Create a SetupIntent when you're ready to collect your customer's payment credentials.
Don't maintain long-lived, unconfirmed SetupIntents because they might not be valid.
The SetupIntent transitions through multiple [statuses](https://docs.stripe.com/payments/intents#intent-statuses) as it guides
you through the setup process.

Successful SetupIntents result in payment credentials that are optimized for future payments.
For example, cardholders in [certain regions](https://stripe.com/guides/strong-customer-authentication) might need to be run through
[Strong Customer Authentication](https://docs.stripe.com/strong-customer-authentication) during payment method collection
to streamline later [off-session payments](https://docs.stripe.com/payments/setup-intents).
If you use the SetupIntent with a [Customer](https://api.stripe.com#setup_intent_object-customer),
it automatically attaches the resulting payment method to that Customer after successful setup.
We recommend using SetupIntents or [setup_future_usage](https://api.stripe.com#payment_intent_object-setup_future_usage) on
PaymentIntents to save payment methods to prevent saving invalid or unoptimized payment methods.

By using SetupIntents, you can reduce friction for your customers, even as regulations change over time.

Related guide: [Setup Intents API](https://docs.stripe.com/payments/setup-intents)
- `source` (String)
- `type` (String) The type of error returned. One of `api_error`, `card_error`, `idempotency_error`, or `invalid_request_error`


<a id="nestedatt--action--collect_inputs"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.collect_inputs`

Read-Only:

- `inputs` (Attributes List) List of inputs to be collected. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs))
- `metadata` (Map of String) Set of [key-value pairs](https://docs.stripe.com/api/metadata) that you can attach to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format.

<a id="nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.collect_inputs.inputs`

Read-Only:

- `custom_text` (Attributes) Default text of input being collected. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs--custom_text))
- `email` (Attributes) Information about a email being collected using a reader (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs--email))
- `numeric` (Attributes) Information about a number being collected using a reader (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs--numeric))
- `phone` (Attributes) Information about a phone number being collected using a reader (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs--phone))
- `required` (Boolean) Indicate that this input is required, disabling the skip button.
- `selection` (Attributes) Information about a selection being collected using a reader (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs--selection))
- `signature` (Attributes) Information about a signature being collected using a reader (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs--signature))
- `skipped` (Boolean) Indicate that this input was skipped by the user.
- `text` (Attributes) Information about text being collected using a reader (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs--text))
- `toggles` (Attributes List) List of toggles being collected. Values are present if collection is complete. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs--toggles))
- `type` (String) Type of input being collected.

<a id="nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs--custom_text"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.collect_inputs.inputs.custom_text`

Read-Only:

- `description` (String) Customize the default description for this input
- `skip_button` (String) Customize the default label for this input's skip button
- `submit_button` (String) Customize the default label for this input's submit button
- `title` (String) Customize the default title for this input


<a id="nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs--email"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.collect_inputs.inputs.email`

Read-Only:

- `value` (String) The collected email address


<a id="nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs--numeric"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.collect_inputs.inputs.numeric`

Read-Only:

- `value` (String) The collected number


<a id="nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs--phone"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.collect_inputs.inputs.phone`

Read-Only:

- `value` (String) The collected phone number


<a id="nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs--selection"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.collect_inputs.inputs.selection`

Read-Only:

- `choices` (Attributes List) List of possible choices to be selected (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs--selection--choices))
- `id` (String) The id of the selected choice
- `text` (String) The text of the selected choice

<a id="nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs--selection--choices"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.collect_inputs.inputs.selection.choices`

Read-Only:

- `id` (String) The identifier for the selected choice. Maximum 50 characters.
- `style` (String) The button style for the choice. Can be `primary` or `secondary`.
- `text` (String) The text to be selected. Maximum 30 characters.



<a id="nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs--signature"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.collect_inputs.inputs.signature`

Read-Only:

- `value` (String) The File ID of a collected signature image


<a id="nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs--text"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.collect_inputs.inputs.text`

Read-Only:

- `value` (String) The collected text value


<a id="nestedatt--action--collect_inputs--inputs--toggles"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.collect_inputs.inputs.toggles`

Read-Only:

- `default_value` (String) The toggle's default value. Can be `enabled` or `disabled`.
- `description` (String) The toggle's description text. Maximum 50 characters.
- `title` (String) The toggle's title text. Maximum 50 characters.
- `value` (String) The toggle's collected value. Can be `enabled` or `disabled`.




<a id="nestedatt--action--collect_payment_method"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.collect_payment_method`

Read-Only:

- `collect_config` (Attributes) Represents a per-transaction override of a reader configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--collect_payment_method--collect_config))
- `payment_intent` (String) Most recent PaymentIntent processed by the reader.
- `payment_method` (String) PaymentMethod objects represent your customer's payment instruments.
You can use them with [PaymentIntents](https://docs.stripe.com/payments/payment-intents) to collect payments or save them to
Customer objects to store instrument details for future payments.

Related guides: [Payment Methods](https://docs.stripe.com/payments/payment-methods) and [More Payment Scenarios](https://docs.stripe.com/payments/more-payment-scenarios).

<a id="nestedatt--action--collect_payment_method--collect_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.collect_payment_method.collect_config`

Read-Only:

- `enable_customer_cancellation` (Boolean) Enable customer-initiated cancellation when processing this payment.
- `skip_tipping` (Boolean) Override showing a tipping selection screen on this transaction.
- `tipping` (Attributes) Represents a per-transaction tipping configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--collect_payment_method--collect_config--tipping))

<a id="nestedatt--action--collect_payment_method--collect_config--tipping"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.collect_payment_method.collect_config.tipping`

Read-Only:

- `amount_eligible` (Number) Amount used to calculate tip suggestions on tipping selection screen for this transaction. Must be a positive integer in the smallest currency unit (e.g., 100 cents to represent $1.00 or 100 to represent ¥100, a zero-decimal currency).




<a id="nestedatt--action--confirm_payment_intent"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.confirm_payment_intent`

Read-Only:

- `confirm_config` (Attributes) Represents a per-transaction override of a reader configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--confirm_payment_intent--confirm_config))
- `payment_intent` (String) Most recent PaymentIntent processed by the reader.

<a id="nestedatt--action--confirm_payment_intent--confirm_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.confirm_payment_intent.confirm_config`

Read-Only:

- `return_url` (String) If the customer doesn't abandon authenticating the payment, they're redirected to this URL after completion.



<a id="nestedatt--action--print_content"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.print_content`

Read-Only:

- `image` (Attributes) Metadata of an uploaded file (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--print_content--image))
- `type` (String) The type of content to print. Currently supports `image`.

<a id="nestedatt--action--print_content--image"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.print_content.image`

Read-Only:

- `created_at` (Number) Creation time of the object (in seconds since the Unix epoch).
- `filename` (String) The original name of the uploaded file (e.g. `receipt.png`).
- `size` (Number) The size (in bytes) of the uploaded file.
- `type` (String) The format of the uploaded file.



<a id="nestedatt--action--process_payment_intent"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.process_payment_intent`

Read-Only:

- `payment_intent` (String) Most recent PaymentIntent processed by the reader.
- `process_config` (Attributes) Represents a per-transaction override of a reader configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--process_payment_intent--process_config))

<a id="nestedatt--action--process_payment_intent--process_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.process_payment_intent.process_config`

Read-Only:

- `enable_customer_cancellation` (Boolean) Enable customer-initiated cancellation when processing this payment.
- `return_url` (String) If the customer doesn't abandon authenticating the payment, they're redirected to this URL after completion.
- `skip_tipping` (Boolean) Override showing a tipping selection screen on this transaction.
- `tipping` (Attributes) Represents a per-transaction tipping configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--process_payment_intent--process_config--tipping))

<a id="nestedatt--action--process_payment_intent--process_config--tipping"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.process_payment_intent.process_config.tipping`

Read-Only:

- `amount_eligible` (Number) Amount used to calculate tip suggestions on tipping selection screen for this transaction. Must be a positive integer in the smallest currency unit (e.g., 100 cents to represent $1.00 or 100 to represent ¥100, a zero-decimal currency).




<a id="nestedatt--action--process_setup_intent"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.process_setup_intent`

Read-Only:

- `generated_card` (String) ID of a card PaymentMethod generated from the card_present PaymentMethod that may be attached to a Customer for future transactions. Only present if it was possible to generate a card PaymentMethod.
- `process_config` (Attributes) Represents a per-setup override of a reader configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--process_setup_intent--process_config))
- `setup_intent` (String) Most recent SetupIntent processed by the reader.

<a id="nestedatt--action--process_setup_intent--process_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.process_setup_intent.process_config`

Read-Only:

- `enable_customer_cancellation` (Boolean) Enable customer-initiated cancellation when processing this SetupIntent.



<a id="nestedatt--action--refund_payment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.refund_payment`

Read-Only:

- `amount` (Number) The amount being refunded.
- `charge` (String) Charge that is being refunded.
- `metadata` (Map of String) Set of [key-value pairs](https://docs.stripe.com/api/metadata) that you can attach to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format.
- `payment_intent` (String) Payment intent that is being refunded.
- `reason` (String) The reason for the refund.
- `refund` (String) Unique identifier for the refund object.
- `refund_application_fee` (Boolean) Boolean indicating whether the application fee should be refunded when refunding this charge. If a full charge refund is given, the full application fee will be refunded. Otherwise, the application fee will be refunded in an amount proportional to the amount of the charge refunded. An application fee can be refunded only by the application that created the charge.
- `refund_payment_config` (Attributes) Represents a per-transaction override of a reader configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--refund_payment--refund_payment_config))
- `reverse_transfer` (Boolean) Boolean indicating whether the transfer should be reversed when refunding this charge. The transfer will be reversed proportionally to the amount being refunded (either the entire or partial amount). A transfer can be reversed only by the application that created the charge.

<a id="nestedatt--action--refund_payment--refund_payment_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.refund_payment.refund_payment_config`

Read-Only:

- `enable_customer_cancellation` (Boolean) Enable customer-initiated cancellation when refunding this payment.



<a id="nestedatt--action--set_reader_display"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.set_reader_display`

Read-Only:

- `cart` (Attributes) Cart object to be displayed by the reader, including line items, amounts, and currency. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--set_reader_display--cart))
- `type` (String) Type of information to be displayed by the reader. Only `cart` is currently supported.

<a id="nestedatt--action--set_reader_display--cart"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.set_reader_display.cart`

Read-Only:

- `currency` (String) Three-letter [ISO currency code](https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html), in lowercase. Must be a [supported currency](https://stripe.com/docs/currencies).
- `line_items` (Attributes List) List of line items in the cart. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--action--set_reader_display--cart--line_items))
- `tax` (Number) Tax amount for the entire cart. A positive integer in the [smallest currency unit](https://docs.stripe.com/currencies#zero-decimal).
- `total` (Number) Total amount for the entire cart, including tax. A positive integer in the [smallest currency unit](https://docs.stripe.com/currencies#zero-decimal).

<a id="nestedatt--action--set_reader_display--cart--line_items"></a>
### Nested Schema for `action.set_reader_display.cart.line_items`

Read-Only:

- `amount` (Number) The amount of the line item. A positive integer in the [smallest currency unit](https://docs.stripe.com/currencies#zero-decimal).
- `description` (String) Description of the line item.
- `quantity` (Number) The quantity of the line item.
